MWCapture SDK  3.3.1.1556
  • Home
  • Development Library
  • Examples
  • Release Notes
MultiAudioCapture C++

The features are as follows:

  1. capturing audio data using Pro/Eco Capture Family Universal Functions.
  2. previewing captured audio and save as .wav files.
  3. saving captured data in LPMC format.
  4. automatically saving the audio data to a file with its type suffix for the compressed audio data. For example, if the input audio format is AC3, the captured data file is AudioCapture.ac3.

Folder dir: SDKv3\Examples\VC++\GUI\MultiAudioCapture

Supported Hardware

Pro Capture Family, Eco Capture Family

Features

  1. Capture audio data
  2. Display waveforms of LPCM audio signals in figures, up to 8 channels are supported.
  3. Play LPCM audio
  4. Save captured data in specified format.
  5. Switch the capture channel mid-stream, without exiting the application.

Workflow

The main thread:

  1. To initialize and enumerate all available devices, call: MWCaptureInitInstance, MWGetChannelCount
  2. To find the valid capture card according to the device info,, call: MWGetChannelInfoByIndex
  3. To initialize an audio renderer, call: initilize_dsound
  4. To open the first available channel, call: MWGetDevicePath, MWOpenChannelByPath
  5. To get the valid audio input type, call: MWGetAudioInputSourceArray
  6. To start audio capture, call: MWStartAudioCapture
  7. Create audio process thread.
  8. Enter the main thread.
  9. Close and re-open the channel and audio renderer while switching the capture channel.
  10. To stop recording, then free up system resources and memory, after exiting the application, call: MWCloseChannel, MWCaptureExitInstance

The audio process thread:

  1. To register notification for a frame cashed and audio signal changed, call: MWRegisterNotify
  2. To get status of audio signal, call: MWGetAudioSignalStatus
  3. To capture an audio frame when receiving a notification that a whold video frame has been buffered, call: MWGetNotifyStatus, MWCaptureAudioFrame
  4. To render captured audio and display it visually.

  5. Start recording when click the record to file button.

    1. Convert LPCM to WAV.
    2. For other audio formats (according to IEC 61937):
      1. Locate the Syncword.
      2. Find the audio format according to its header (IEC 61937).
      3. Find the number of bits or bytes for each data-type.
      4. Convert the captured audio data to the specified format.

  6. Close and re-open the rendering task when signal changed.
    1. Close audio rendering.
    2. Obtain input signal format.
    3. Re-start rendering.

Flow Chart

MulitAudioCapture

Result

MultiAudioCapture
